sections.content:
SPACE had no impact on gameplay (only HUD changed). But the board did change (12 cells changed). Let me check what changed. Looking at the image, I see the white line now has a blue center (it's a blue square now). This is the same pattern as before - SPACE toggles between a thin white line and a white strip with a center.
But wait, the result says "gameplay_changed: false" and "no_impact: true". So this change is considered part of the HUD band, not gameplay. But the white line changed from a thin line to a strip with a center. This is confusing.
Let me check the current state to see what actually changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if n['color'] not in ['w', 'g']:
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)}), children={n['children']}")

# Check what changed
if last_transition:
    before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation
    print("\nBefore white line:", [n for n in before['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W'][0])
    print("After white line:", [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W'][0])
    # Check for blue center
    before_blue = [n for n in before['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 4]
    after_blue = [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 4]
    print(f"Before blue centers: {len(before_blue)}, After blue centers: {len(after_blue)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
